feat: migrate game-context.tsx from localStorage to Appwrite (task [2])

Critical security fixes from code review

CRITICAL FIXES:
1. Withdrawal balance now deducted immediately on request
   - Prevents double-withdrawal exploit
   - Balance returned if admin rejects

2. Deposit validation strengthened
   - File upload validation now called
   - Payment method validated (bank/crypto)
   - Crypto type validated against allowed list
   - Case normalization (lowercase payment_method)

3. KYC notifications added
   - Users notified when KYC verified/rejected
   - Rejection reason included in notification

4. Password policy strengthened
   - Minimum 8 characters (was 6)
   - Requires uppercase letter
   - Requires lowercase letter
   - Requires number

5. Simplified get_available_balance()
   - Now returns actual balance since withdrawals deduct immediately

feat: automated changelog and AI-generated release notes pipeline

Add git-cliff for structured changelog generation from conventional
commits, plus an OpenAI-powered release notes script that produces
bilingual (EN/DE), user-friendly summaries with secret-stripping.

Pipeline in release.yml:
1. git-cliff extracts changes since last tag → raw-changelog.md
2. generate-release-notes.ps1 calls OpenAI API → EN + DE summaries
3. CHANGELOG.md is auto-updated and committed back to main
4. GitHub Release body uses the AI summary instead of raw commit list

Graceful fallback: if OPENAI_API_KEY secret is not set, raw changelog
is used directly. No hard dependency on external AI services.

New files:
- cliff.toml: git-cliff config mapping conventional commit types
- scripts/generate-release-notes.ps1: AI summarization with security
  post-filter (strips API keys, internal paths, implementation details)

New required GitHub secret: OPENAI_API_KEY (optional, for AI summaries)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Hook logWarn() into crash reporter (async, severity=warning)

fix(smart-mode): custom template edit/delete buttons not working

Root cause: Inline onclick='event.stopPropagation()' on edit/delete
buttons inside preset cards prevented click events from bubbling to
the document-level delegated event handler in 09-customtemplates.js.

Fix: Attach click handlers directly to buttons via addEventListener
instead of relying on document-level delegation. stopPropagation is
still used to prevent the parent card's onclick (selectSmartPreset)
from firing.

commit.message:
perf(shutdown): parallelize subprocess termination on quit

LLM and STT server stops now run concurrently instead of sequentially.
Each can take up to 5s timeout, so worst-case shutdown drops from
~10s to ~5s. Typical case (one server running) sees no change, but
when both are active the improvement is immediately noticeable.

commit.message:
feat: add azd (Azure Developer CLI) to registry (#8828)

Adds `azd` (Azure Developer CLI) as a short name in the mise registry,
backed by `aqua:Azure/azure-dev`.

This allows users to install azd with:
```
mise use -g azd@latest
```

Azure Developer CLI is a developer-centric CLI for creating and
deploying Azure applications. It's actively maintained at
https://github.com/Azure/azure-dev.

Note: Depends on the aqua registry fix in
https://github.com/aquaproj/aqua-registry/pull/51272 for correct asset
resolution (version prefix, arm64 support).

chore: apply prettier formatting to azure-deploy.yml (#381)

* Initial plan

* chore: apply prettier formatting to azure-deploy.yml

The prettier-yaml pre-commit hook was reformatting the `if` condition
in the `tf-plan` job of `.github/workflows/azure-deploy.yml`.

The condition checking `github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]'` was split
across 3 lines in a way that exceeded the 120-char print-width. Prettier
reformatted it to fit within the limit, joining continuation lines differently.

This commit applies the prettier-generated format so pre-commit passes cleanly.

commit.message:
perf(download): increase buffer to 256KB, add buffered I/O and debounced progress

- Read buffer: 32 KB → 256 KB (8x reduction in read syscalls)
- Write buffer: unbuffered → 256 KB bufio.Writer (batches disk writes)
- Progress callbacks debounced to every 256 KB (was every read)
- HTTP transport tuned: ReadBufferSize, WriteBufferSize, connection pooling
- Added TestDownloadFile and TestDownloadFileNoProgressCallback

For a 500 MB model, this reduces progress callback invocations from
~16,000 to ~2,000 and disk write syscalls by ~8x.
